content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN. Your task is to generate a permutation PPP of size NNN, such that:


For all (1<i≤N)(1\lt i\le N)(1<i≤N), ∑j=1iPj\sum_{j = 1}^{i} P_j∑j=1i​Pj​ is **not divisible** by iii.

In other words, the sum of prefix of length iii (i>1)(i\gt 1)(i>1) should not be divisible by iii.
In case multiple such permutations exist, print any. If no such permutation exists, print −1-1−1 instead.

Note that a permutation of size NNN contains all integers from 111 to NNN exactly once.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists a single integer NNN — the size of the permutation.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, NNN space separated integers, denoting the required permutation.


In case multiple such permutations exist, print any. If no such permutation exists, print −1-1−1 instead.
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases ≤ 2·10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (2 ≤ N ≤ 10^4).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line. If a required permutation exists, output N space‑separated integers – a permutation of {1,…,N} such that for every i (2 ≤ i ≤ N) the sum of the first i numbers is not divisible by i. If no such permutation exists, output -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=4 the permutation 2 1 4 3 satisfies the condition. For N=6 the permutation 2 1 4 3 6 5 works. N=7 is odd, so no permutation exists and -1 is printed.

solution.approach:
The total sum of a permutation of size N is fixed: S_N = N·(N+1)/2. If N is odd, S_N is divisible by N, so the condition for i = N can never be satisfied – answer is -1. For even N we can always construct a valid permutation by swapping each adjacent pair: (2,1,4,3,6,5,…,N,N-1). For any even i the prefix sum equals (i/2)*(i+1) which leaves remainder i/2 ≠ 0 modulo i. For odd i the prefix contains (i‑1)/2 full swapped pairs plus the first element of the next pair, i+1, giving a sum that is never a multiple of i. Hence this simple construction works for all even N.
Algorithm:
1. Read T.
2. For each test case read N.
3. If N is odd, print -1.
4. Otherwise, for k from 1 to N step 2 output k+1 followed by k.
Complexities: Time O(N) per test case, total O(∑N). Space O(1) besides output buffer.
solution.timeComplexity: O(∑N) – linear in the total size of all test cases.
